body.title: Non-Claude worker: tiered escalation baked into system prompt
body.text:
# D-minion-worker-escalation-2026-05-31
**title:** Non-Claude worker: tiered escalation baked into system prompt
**date:** 2026-05-31
**status:** open
**spawned_tickets:** T-minion-worker-device, T-granny-worker-dispatch, T-consequence-D-minion-escalation
**goal_link:** cost-reduction + capabilities-expansion (self-funding platform)
**concept_links:** none

## Decision narrative
Build a minion-tier rack device (devices/minion/) that executes sprint tickets using cheap inference (DeepSeek V4 Flash ~$0.10/1M) instead of spawning Claude CLI. The system prompt bakes in a tiered escalation contract: worker responds with DONE (commit+close) or ESCALATE: worker / analyst / designer (re-route via RulesEngine with escalation notes as context). Granny's dispatch switches from tmux+claude to comms://worker-inbox. Goal: cost reduction + capabilities expansion toward a self-funding platform with reach beyond Igor.

## Hypothesis
Sprint tickets complete via cheap models without touching Claude; platform becomes useful for non-Igor work, expanding reach.

## Measurement Signal
InferenceDevice budget ledger shows spend against openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash for closed tickets; escalation tier distribution log shows what % of tickets reach analyst/designer.

## Goal Link
cost-reduction + capabilities-expansion — making the platform self-funding by routing minion-class work to $0.10/1M models

## Concept Links
none

## Provenance
Absorbed from the TheIgors.archive lineage 2026-06-28 (D-all-generations-consolidation-2026-06-28). Historical record; original status: open.
